name: Constance Smith-Hicks
expertise: Runs one of only two SYNGAP1 Centers of Excellence. Provides comprehensive clinical care for SYNGAP1 patients.

name: Gavin Rumbaugh
expertise: Devoted entire lab to SYNGAP1 translational research. Developing first potential oral pill that boosts SynGAP protein expression. $7.7M NIMH grant.

name: Ingrid Scheffer
expertise: Pioneer in epilepsy genetics who found the first gene implicated in epilepsy. Co-authored landmark SYNGAP1 mutations paper.

name: Jimmy L. Holder Jr.
expertise: Runs the other SYNGAP1 Center of Excellence. Over 94% of his SYNGAP1 patients have epilepsy.

name: Kelly Knupp
expertise: Founding member of Pediatric Epilepsy Research Consortium. Board Chair of Dravet Syndrome Foundation. Expert in epileptic encephalopathies including ESES.

name: Prakash Kotagal
expertise: Authored definitive meta-analysis of 575 ESES cases. Established steroids 81% and surgery 90% as most effective treatments.

name: Renee Shellhaas
expertise: Led multicenter study across 11 US centers on CSWS treatment practices and outcomes. Found steroids and benzodiazepines outperform standard ASMs.

name: Richard Huganir
expertise: Discovered the SynGAP gene/protein in 1998. Chair of SRF Scientific Advisory Board. His lab is the foundational SYNGAP1 research lab worldwide.

name: Ajay Gupta
expertise: Head of Pediatric Epilepsy. Published research on electroclinical presentation and treatment of children with ESES.

name: Bart van den Munckhof
expertise: Co-led RESCUE ESES trial. Published largest pooled analysis of ESES treatments (575 cases). World's leading ESES treatment researcher.

name: Elaine Wirrell
expertise: Director of Pediatric Epilepsy at Mayo. Chair of ILAE Pediatric Commission. 300+ publications.

name: Eric Kossoff
expertise: Published specific research on ketogenic diet for ESES (53% EEG improvement, 45% cognitive improvement). World's premier KD program.

name: Helen Cross
expertise: ILAE President. Involved in RESCUE ESES trial. Coordinator of EpiCARE. Leading global authority on childhood epilepsy.

name: James Riviello
expertise: Internationally recognized expert specifically in LKS and ESES. 2023 Lifetime Achievement Award from Child Neurology Society.

name: Joseph Sullivan
expertise: Director of UCSF Pediatric Epilepsy Center of Excellence. PI for numerous clinical trials in pediatric epilepsy.

name: Kees Braun
expertise: THE world authority on ESES treatment. Led RESCUE ESES trial showing 84% improvement with corticosteroids. Professor of Paediatric Neurology.

name: Lekha Rao
expertise: Explicitly listed expertise in CSWS, LKS, and ESES. One of few who specifically names ESES/CSWS as primary clinical focus.

name: Megan Abbott
expertise: PI for SYNGAP1 Clinic at CHCO. Awarded $450K SRF grant to establish Clinical Research Center of Excellence.

name: Phillip Pearl
expertise: Director of Epilepsy and Clinical Neurophysiology. William G. Lennox Chair in Neurology. Leads top-ranked pediatric epilepsy program.

name: Rima Nabbout
expertise: Head of Reference Center for Rare Epilepsies at Necker Hospital. PI on fenfluramine trials. Expert in precision medicine for epilepsy.

name: Robert Stowe
expertise: Specific focus on sleep, epilepsy, and genetics interface. Explicit expertise in ESES and Landau-Kleffner syndrome.

name: Tobias Loddenkemper
expertise: One of the world's foremost ESES/CSWS researchers. Authored landmark surveys on ESES/CSWS terminology and treatment across North America.

name: Tommaso Lo Barco
expertise: One of foremost European SYNGAP1 researchers. Identified fixation-off and eye closure as key seizure triggers.

name: William Gaillard
expertise: Past President of AES. Past Chair of ILAE Commission on Diagnostics. 240+ peer-reviewed papers.

name: Annapurna Poduri
expertise: Published landmark 2024 Epilepsia paper on SYNGAP1 phenotypes (147 patients, 84% epilepsy). Former Deputy Director of NINDS.

name: Ingo Helbig
expertise: Director of Genomic Science at ENGIN. PI on SYNGAP1 Natural History Study. Runs one of world's largest epilepsy neurogenetics programs.

name: Juliet Knowles
expertise: Co-director of Stanford Synaptopathy Clinic. PI of ProMMiS. Investigating novel SYNGAP1 pathological mechanisms. SRF-funded $130K grant.

name: Alexis Arzimanoglou
expertise: Coordinator of EpiCARE. Leading expert on ESES cognitive outcomes. Co-investigator on RESCUE ESES
paperTitle: Cognitive impairment in ESES

name: Benjamin Prosser
expertise: Director of ENDD center developing ASO therapies specifically for SYNGAP1
paperTitle: Mapping PTBP2 binding identifies SYNGAP1 as target for therapeutic splice switching

name: Beverly Davidson
expertise: Director of Perelman Center for Cellular and Molecular Therapeutics at CHOP. Co-director of ENDD developing gene therapies for SYNGAP1
paperTitle: ENDD Center therapeutic development

name: Boaz Levi
expertise: First successful demonstration of full-length SYNGAP1 gene delivery via AAV. Nearly eliminated epileptic brain activity in mice
paperTitle: AAV gene supplementation therapy for SYNGAP1

name: Daniel Tardiff
expertise: CSO of CAMP4 Therapeutics. CMP-SYNGAP-01 entering Phase 1/2 H2 2026 — closest SYNGAP1-specific drug to clinic. $100M raised

name: Danique Vlaskamp
expertise: Lead author of definitive 57-patient SYNGAP1 encephalopathy study identifying distinctive seizure types
paperTitle: SYNGAP1 encephalopathy: A distinctive generalized DEE

name: Elizabeth Heller
expertise: Developed mutation-independent CRISPRa approach that upregulates functional SYNGAP1 allele and rescued memory deficits in mice
paperTitle: CRISPRa-based transcriptional activation for SYNGAP1

name: Federico Vigevano
expertise: Investigator on the ONLY trial testing fenfluramine in both SYNGAP1 AND CSWS simultaneously
paperTitle: Fenfluramine for DEEs including SYNGAP1 and CSWS (NCT05232630)

name: Floor Jansen
expertise: PI of RESCUE ESES, the landmark European RCT. Found corticosteroids produced IQ improvement of 11+ points in 25% of patients
paperTitle: RESCUE ESES RCT

name: Heather Mefford
expertise: Identified SYNGAP1 as a cause of epileptic encephalopathies. 2024 AES Basic Science Research Award
paperTitle: Targeted resequencing identifies de novo mutations in CHD2 and SYNGAP1
journal: CHD2 and SYNGAP1" in Nature Genetics

name: Jillian McKee
expertise: ENGIN epileptologist at CHOP. Uses multi-omics and ML to understand SYNGAP1-RD disease trajectories
paperTitle: ProMMiS Natural History Study

name: Kimberly Wiltrout
expertise: Led largest phenotypic study of SYNGAP1 (147 patients), finding 84% epilepsy and 68% autism rates

expertise: Chair of ILAE Task Force that defined DEE-SWAS as a syndrome. 280+ publications

expertise: Senior co-author on landmark SYNGAP1 encephalopathy study. World-renowned pediatric epileptologist
paperTitle: SYNGAP1 encephalopathy

expertise: PI of the only active US clinical trial specifically targeting ESES with novel therapy. Treated ~300 ESES patients

expertise: Site PI for ProMMiS at Children's Hospital Colorado. Researches disease-modifying treatments for neurogenetic conditions
paperTitle: ProMMiS site PI

name: Anup Patel
expertise: Director of Epilepsy Center. Co-first author on NEJM Epidiolex for LGS publication.

name: Daniel Lowenstein
expertise: Former AES President. Created NINDS Epilepsy Research Benchmarks guiding federal research priorities.

name: Dennis Dlugos
expertise: Expert in epilepsy genetics and pharmacogenetics. Director of Section of Clinical Neurophysiology at CHOP.

name: Jack Parent
expertise: Co-Chair of DSF Scientific Advisory Board. Co-director of Comprehensive Epilepsy Center at University of Michigan.

name: Lori Isom
expertise: Co-Chair of DSF Scientific Advisory Board. Research into sodium channel dysfunction in epilepsy.

name: Sameer Zuberi
expertise: Former EPNS President. Clinical Lead of Scottish National Genetic Epilepsy Service. ILAE Classification Commission.

name: Vicky Whittemore
expertise: Program Director at NINDS overseeing NIH epilepsy grants portfolio. Key federal gatekeeper for epilepsy research funding.

name: Angela Kaindl
expertise: Heads German Epilepsy Center for Children at Charite. Center explicitly treats DEE with SWAS. RESCUE ESES participant.

name: Carl Stafstrom
expertise: Director of Pediatric Neurology at Hopkins. 400+ publications. AES Founders Award recipient.

name: Cigdem Akman
expertise: Medical Director of Pediatric Epilepsy Surgery at NYU. Former Director at Columbia.

name: Edward Novotny
expertise: Director of Epilepsy Program at Seattle Children's. 25+ years treating children with epilepsy.

name: Eija Gaily
expertise: RESCUE ESES trial collaborator. Published long-term outcome study of 32 children with ESES.

name: Georgia Ramantani
expertise: Head of EEG/Epilepsy Division. Co-author on 2026 DEE-SWAS treatment study.

name: Hirokazu Oguni
expertise: Leading Japanese authority on pediatric epilepsy including absence and atypical absence epilepsy.

name: John Millichap
expertise: Runs Precision Epilepsy PLLC telehealth practice. 60+ publications. Makes genetic epilepsy expertise accessible remotely.

name: Josiane Lajoie
expertise: Director of Pediatric Epilepsy Program at NYU. 20+ years experience.

name: Katherine Holland-Bouley
expertise: Medical Director of Comprehensive Epilepsy Center at Cincinnati Children's.

name: Lieven Lagae
expertise: Co-Director of Leuven Childhood Epilepsy Center. EpiCARE reference center. Decades of experience.

name: Linda Laux
expertise: Section Head of Epilepsy Center. Board Chair of Dravet Syndrome Foundation. Co-leading gene regulation therapy clinical trials.

name: Mary Connolly
expertise: Director of Epilepsy Program at BC Children's. Comprehensive clinic treating genetic epilepsy syndromes including DEEs.

name: Se Hee Kim
expertise: Published on DEE-SWAS treatment outcomes including steroid therapy and ketogenic diet effectiveness.

name: Shaun Hussain
expertise: Director of UCLA Infantile Spasms Program. Studies autism-epilepsy connection directly relevant to SYNGAP1 cases.

name: Stephane Auvin
expertise: Director of Rare Epilepsy Center at Robert-Debre. ILAE Ambassador Award 2023. Leads regulatory affairs task force.

name: Sudha Kessler
expertise: Program Director of CHOP Epilepsy. Catherine D. Brown Endowed Chair. Research on DEE with spike-wave activation in sleep.

name: Tracy Glauser
expertise: Led landmark NIH-funded Childhood Absence Epilepsy trial (32 centers, 446 patients). Deep expertise in pharmacogenetics.

name: Gaetan Lesca
expertise: Leads molecular diagnosis of epilepsies in Lyon. Co-authored major SYNGAP1 spectrum paper.

name: Anne de Saint-Martin
expertise: Published critical long-term outcome data for CSWS. Co-investigator on RESCUE ESES

expertise: Published most recent comprehensive review of CSWS (2024)

expertise: Develops novel treatments for epileptic encephalopathies including disease-modifying approaches

name: Erin Heinzen
expertise: Contributed to discovery of 15 novel epilepsy genes. Bridges genetics and treatment for precision medicine

name: Gabriele Lignani
expertise: Developing CRISPR-based gene therapy tools applicable to SYNGAP1 and other DEEs. Won ILAE's top three awards (2021-2024)

expertise: Published longitudinal data on SYNGAP1-DEE patients showing condition evolution over time

paperTitle: AAV delivery of full-length SYNGAP1 rescues phenotypes
